title: Repo & Org Structure
description: This view shows how AperixStudio should be organised so your agency site, internal admin, templates, and client sites stay cleanly separated.
noteBody: Demo work lives in Aperix-Website. Real paid client work gets its own repo immediately.

layout.title: Recommended GitHub Org Layout
layout.heading: AperixStudio
layout.tree:
AperixStudio/
├─ Aperix-Website
│  ├─ public agency website
│  ├─ demos / portfolio pieces
│  └─ lead capture / enquiry flow
│
├─ Aperix-Admin
│  ├─ internal dashboard
│  ├─ client records
│  ├─ health checks
│  └─ hosting / repo / access links
│
├─ aperix-site-starter
│  ├─ reusable Next.js starter
│  ├─ shared blocks and patterns
│  └─ launch baseline for new clients
│
├─ client-rhinos-walk-site
├─ client-future-cafe-site
├─ client-future-tradie-site
│
└─ aperix-internal
   ├─ SOPs
   ├─ docs
   └─ small scripts / operations

items.title: Aperix-Website
items.description: Public marketing site, live demos, portfolio, pricing, and contact funnel.

items.title: Aperix-Admin
items.description: Private command center for both of you to manage operational reality.

items.title: aperix-site-starter
items.description: Starter base for new builds so you stop rebuilding everything from zero.

clientPattern.title: Client repo pattern
clientPattern.heading: Example: client-rhinos-walk-site
clientPattern.tree:
client-rhinos-walk-site/
├─ app/
├─ components/
├─ lib/
├─ public/
├─ docs/
│  ├─ content-notes
│  ├─ launch-checklist
│  └─ handoff-notes
├─ .env.example
├─ netlify.toml
└─ README (optional internal use)

items.title: Lead enters through Aperix-Website
items.description: Contact form captures the enquiry and later feeds a client/project record into the admin app.

items.title: Project is accepted
items.description: Create a new client repo from aperix-site-starter and assign lead/support owners.

items.title: Site gets hosted
items.description: Connect GitHub, Netlify, Cloudflare, and the domain registrar, then document the stack in admin.

items.title: Admin app becomes the control layer
items.description: It does not replace the repos — it sits above them and gives you one operational view.

rows.problem: Client work mixed into agency site
Hard to know where hosting lives
rows.fix: Admin links each project to its stack
Lead and support roles are explicit

design.title: Design takeaway
design.callout: This page is less about code browsing and more about architecture clarity: what repos exist, what they are for, and how they connect to domains, hosts, and ownership.
